skill.name: using-email-templates
skill.description: Guides agents through creating, testing, and sending Mailtrap-hosted email templates with template UUIDs and Handlebars variables.
skill.summary: Create, test, and send Mailtrap email templates with API payload guidance.
skill.author: mailtrap

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many backtick and weak-crypto patterns, but review found Markdown prose, inline code spans, and documentation examples rather than executable code or cryptography. The confirmed risk is legitimate network email sending guidance that uses a Mailtrap API token and can send transactional or bulk messages if a user runs the example against live Mailtrap endpoints.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Live Email Sending Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill documents Mailtrap live send endpoints, an Authorization bearer token, and a curl POST request for sending a template email. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it can send real email or bulk messages if used with a valid token, so users should prefer sandbox testing before live sends.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Mailtrap send endpoints and bearer token header are explicitly documented with a POST example. The behavior is intended and transparent, so the concern is misuse or accidental live sending rather than malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution patterns are Markdown inline code spans, documentation references, and a fenced curl example. No evidence found of executable Ruby code, dynamic command execution, or user-controlled shell interpolation in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are visible Markdown text and shell documentation, not source code that the skill executes. The only command shown is a user-run curl example for the documented Mailtrap API.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Prose False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography alerts point to YAML description text and not to cryptographic functions, algorithms, keys, or hashing code. No evidence found of weak cryptographic implementation in the reviewed skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain only the skill description about Mailtrap templates and previews. There is no crypto-related code or configuration at those locations.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Official Documentation and Service Endpoints
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to Mailtrap documentation, Mailtrap API hosts, and official Mailtrap SDK repositories. These links are expected for a Mailtrap integration skill and do not show data exfiltration to unrelated domains.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The domains are Mailtrap documentation, Mailtrap API hosts, and Mailtrap GitHub repositories. The network targets match the declared purpose of the skill.

content.value_statement: Email template integrations often fail because payload fields, variables, and stream endpoints are mixed. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Mailtrap template sends, previews, and SDK research.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Mailtrap-hosted templates with template_uuid and template_variables.
Points agents to official Mailtrap docs for transactional, bulk, sandbox, and template APIs.
Shows the required Authorization bearer token pattern for Mailtrap send requests.
Summarizes supported Handlebars patterns for variables, conditionals, loops, and nested paths.
Guides generation of responsive email template HTML for Mailtrap-hosted templates.
Links to official Mailtrap SDK repositories for language-specific implementation checks.
limitations: It does not include executable helper scripts or validation tools.
It requires current Mailtrap docs for exact SDK method signatures.
It does not manage API tokens or Mailtrap account permissions.
It cannot verify template rendering without access to Mailtrap preview or sandbox tools.

use_cases.title: Add Template Sends to an App
use_cases.description: Create a Mailtrap API request that sends an existing template with the correct UUID and variables.

use_cases.title: Prepare Responsive Email Templates
use_cases.description: Generate mobile-compatible email HTML that can be pasted into a Mailtrap-hosted template.

use_cases.title: Debug Template Variables
use_cases.description: Check whether payload variables match Handlebars names and preview the result before live sending.

prompt_templates.title: Send an Existing Template
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for one transactional template email.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me send a Mailtrap-hosted email template. I have a template UUID, recipient address, sender address, and template variables.

prompt_templates.title: Map Variables to Handlebars
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate check for template data and personalization.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Mailtrap template variable names and payload values. Identify missing, unused, or unsafe Handlebars variables before I test the email.

prompt_templates.title: Create Responsive Template HTML
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate content creation for a new hosted template.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate responsive email template HTML for Mailtrap. Use Handlebars variables for the recipient name, order details, and call to action.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Bulk Template Integration
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for promotional sends and SDK research.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a Mailtrap bulk template send integration. Include the correct stream choice, payload fields, sandbox testing steps, and SDK documentation checks.

output_examples.input: I need to send a welcome email from a Mailtrap template.
output_examples.output: A concise integration plan with the template UUID, sender, recipient, variable list, API endpoint choice, and a sandbox-first testing checklist.

output_examples.input: My template does not show the customer name.
output_examples.output: A variable mismatch review that compares the Handlebars placeholder with the payload key and suggests the corrected variable name.

output_examples.input: Create a new receipt template for Mailtrap.
output_examples.output: Responsive email template content with sections for receipt details, totals, support information, and safe Handlebars placeholders.

best_practices: Read current Mailtrap API or SDK docs before generating final integration code.
Use sandbox or preview testing before sending messages through live transactional or bulk streams.
Keep template UUIDs, variable names, and stream endpoints explicit in implementation notes.
anti_patterns: Do not mix raw text or HTML body fields with template_uuid in the same send payload.
Do not place Mailtrap API tokens directly in source code or shared prompts.
Do not use raw Handlebars HTML output unless the content is trusted and reviewed.

faq.question: Does this skill send email by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. A user or agent must run an API request with a valid Mailtrap token.

faq.question: Can it create Mailtrap templates?
faq.answer: It can guide template creation in the Mailtrap UI or through the Templates API documentation.

faq.question: Which template engine does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Mailtrap Handlebars patterns, including variables, conditionals, loops, nested paths, and escaped output.

faq.question: Can it help with SDK code?
faq.answer: Yes, but it instructs agents to read the current official SDK README before generating method-specific code.

faq.question: Is it safe for production email sends?
faq.answer: It can support production workflows, but users should test with sandbox or preview tools before live delivery.

faq.question: Does it store API tokens?
faq.answer: No. It references bearer token usage but does not store, request, or manage secrets.
